title: Sample means and the central limit theorem
description: Practice using the central limit theorem to describe the shape of the sampling distribution of a sample mean.
topic_path: Sampling distribution of a sample mean
Sample means and the central limit theorem

assessment_items.question_content:
Patients recovering from a stroke have their grip strengths measured in each hand to monitor their progress. A certain population of over $100$ male patients have grip strengths in their dominant hands with a mean and standard deviation of approximately $41\,\text{kg}$ and $9\,\text{kg}$, respectively.

Suppose that we take random samples of $4$ male patients from this population and calculate $\bar{x}$ as the sample mean grip strength from each group of patients.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size. Sample means coming from large samples ($n\geq30$) will be normally distributed, but sample means coming from small samples ($n<30$) may not necessarily be normal.

We aren't told anything about the shape of the population distribution of grip strengths, and our sample size of $4$ adults is small ($4<30$), so we don't know what shape the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will have.

###Answer

The shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ is unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
A quality control manager at an auto plant measures the paint thickness on newly painted cars. A certain part that they paint has a mean thickness of $2\,\text{mm}$ with a standard deviation of $0.8\,\text{mm}$.

The manager takes a daily random sample of thickness measurements from $100$ randomly chosen points on these parts and calculates the sample mean thickness $\bar x$ from each sample. We can assume that the points in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size. Sample means coming from large samples ($n\geq30$) will be normally distributed, but sample means coming from small samples ($n<30$) may not necessarily be normal.

We aren't told anything about the shape of the population distribution of thickness measurements, but our sample size of $100$ measurements is large ($100\geq30$), so the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be normally distributed.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
A large pizza chain tracks how many pizzas each customer buys per order. The number of pizzas customers buy in any given order has a mean of $2.5$ pizzas and a standard deviation of  $1.5$ pizzas.

Suppose that we take random samples of $7$ orders from this population and calculate $\bar{x}$ as the sample mean number of pizzas purchased per order. We can assume that the orders in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size. Sample means coming from large samples ($n\geq30$) will be normally distributed, but sample means coming from small samples ($n<30$) may not necessarily be normal.

We aren't told anything about the shape of the population distribution of how many pizzas are purchased per order, and our sample size of $7$ orders is small ($7<30$), so we don't know what shape the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will have.

###Answer

The shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ is unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
A tennis shop uses a machine to string tennis racquets to specific tensions requested by their customers. There's variability in the results, so when the machine is set to string at a tension of $60$ pounds, the resulting racquets have a mean tension of $60$ pounds with a standard deviation of $0.5$ pounds.

Suppose that we took random samples of $36$ racquets and calculated the sample mean tension from each sample. We can assume that the racquets in each sample are independent.

**What would be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean tension?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size. Sample means coming from large samples ($n\geq30$) will be normally distributed, but sample means coming from small samples ($n<30$) may not necessarily be normal.

We aren't told anything about the shape of the population distribution of tension measurements, but our sample size of $36$ racquets is large ($36\geq30$), so the sampling distribution of the sample mean tension will be normally distributed.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean tension will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
A team of employees at a company has daily meetings that are scheduled to take $30$ minutes, and sometimes they go for the full time, but they often only last a few minutes. The mean duration of these meetings is $20$ minutes and the standard deviation is $10$ minutes.

Suppose that we take random samples of $5$ meetings from this population and calculate $\bar{x}$ as the sample mean duration of each sample of meetings. We can assume that the meetings in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size. Sample means coming from large samples ($n\geq30$) will be normally distributed, but sample means coming from small samples ($n<30$) may not necessarily be normal.

We aren't told anything about the shape of the population distribution of these meeting durations, and our sample size of $5$ meetings is small ($5<30$), so we don't know what shape the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will have.

###Answer

The shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ is unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
Fuel prices in Vancouver one month had a mean price of $\text{C}\$1.34$ per liter and a standard deviation of $\text{C}\$0.36$ per liter.

Suppose that we take random samples of $45$ prices from this population and calculate the sample mean price for each sample. We can assume that the prices in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean price?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size. Sample means coming from large samples ($n\geq30$) will be normally distributed, but sample means coming from small samples ($n<30$) may not necessarily be normal.

We aren't told anything about the shape of the population distribution of prices, but our sample size of $45$ prices is large ($45\geq30$), so the sampling distribution of the sample mean price will be normally distributed.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean price will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape.

assessment_items.question_content:
A car manufacturer crash tests a certain model of car and measures the impact force. The test and model in question produce impact forces that are normally distributed with a mean of $30$ metric tons and a standard deviation of $1.5$ metric tons. Suppose that the manufacturer tests a random sample of $3$ cars and calculates the sample mean impact force. Assume that the cars in the sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean impact force?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

Since the distribution of impact forces is normal, the sampling distribution of the sample mean impact force will also be normal.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean impact force will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
The wingspans of a common species of housefly are normally distributed with a mean of $15\,\text{mm}$ and a standard deviation of $0.5\,\text{mm}$. Suppose that a biologist regularly collects random samples of $20$ of these houseflies and calculates the sample mean wingspan from each sample.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean wingspan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

Since the distribution of wingspans is normal, the sampling distribution of the sample mean wingspan will also be normal.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean wingspan will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
A certain type of golfball is tested by a robot that hits the ball with a consistent impact force. The distances this type of ball travels in this test are normally distributed with a mean of $195$ meters and a standard deviation of $5$ meters. Suppose that quality control experts regularly collect random samples of $25$ distance measurements and calculate the sample mean distance in each sample. Assume that the measurements in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean distance?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

Since the distribution of distances is normal, the sampling distribution of the sample mean distance will also be normal.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean distance will be approximately normal.

A large airliner offers the same flight every day between two major airports. The daily number of passengers on this flight follows a normal distribution similar to this:

Suppose that the airliner takes a random sample of $7$ days and calculates the sample mean of the number of passengers for those days.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

Since the distribution of daily number of passengers is normal, the sampling distribution of the sample mean number of passengers will also be normal.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean number of passengers will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
A customer service department monitors how long their callers wait on hold before being helped. The time spent by the callers on hold follows a normal distribution similar to this:

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus time on hold in minutes. The x axis goes from 0 to 8 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.40. The distribution is symmetrical between 0 and 8 with a mean frequency of 0.40 at approximately 4.5 minutes.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/28733aebbee9df55e923ef9dffe2d6645a8c44cd)

Suppose that we took random samples of $15$ callers and calculated the sample mean time spent on hold for each sample of customers. We can assume that the callers in each sample are independent.

**What would be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

Since the distribution of time spent by callers on hold is normal, the sampling distribution of the sample mean time would also be normal.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean time spent by callers on hold would be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Employees at a hospital take the temperature of every one of its more than $1{,}000$ patients who visit the emergency room. The body temperatures of these patients follow a normal distribution similar to this:

![A normal distribution curve is plotted on an x axis representing body temperature in degrees Celsius which goes from 36.1 to 37.5. The curve has a mean of 36.8 degrees.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4f66f5108621d257e9f8013d7a84b65e6639c6e6)

Suppose that the hospital takes a random sample of $16$ patients and calculates the sample mean temperature for those patients.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean temperature?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ will also be normal regardless of sample size.

Since the distribution of temperatures is normal, the sampling distribution of the sample mean temperature will also be normal.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean temperature will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Houseflies have pretty short lifespans. Males of a certain species have lifespans that are strongly skewed to the right with a mean of $26$ days and a standard deviation of $12$ days. A biologist collects a random sample of $10$ of these male houseflies and observes them to calculate the sample mean lifespan. We can assume that the houseflies in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean lifespan?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Since the population of lifespans is skewed to the right, and our sample size of $10$ houseflies is small $(n<30)$, the sampling distribution of the sample mean will still be skewed right but will be approaching a normal distribution.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean lifespan will be skewed to the right, but less skewed than the population.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Male mosquitos have pretty short lifespans. Males of a certain species have lifespans that are strongly skewed to the right with a mean of $8$ days and a standard deviation of $6$ days. A biologist collects a random sample of $50$ of these male mosquitos and observes them to calculate the sample mean lifespan. We can assume that the mosquitos in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean lifespan?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.
Even though the parent population is skewed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal since our sample size of $50$ mosquitos is large $(n \geq 30)$.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean lifespan will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
A group of teachers decided that they would each bring a similar jar of candies to their classrooms so their students could practice estimation. The students were told that whoever had the closest guess in the class would win the candy, and so they all guessed how many candies were in their classroom's jar. The distribution of individual guesses was strongly skewed to the right with a mean of $115$ candies and a standard deviation of $35$ candies.

Suppose we took random samples of $6$ students and calculated $\bar x$ as the sample mean of their guesses. We can assume that the students in each sample are independent.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Since the population of guesses is skewed to the right, and our sample size of $6$ students is small $(n<30)$, the sampling distribution of the sample mean will still be skewed right but will be approaching a normal distribution.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of $\bar x$ will be skewed to the right, but less skewed than the population.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Ms. Martino always gives a very easy $10$-question quiz in the first week of her classes. Over the years, the number of questions that students answer correctly on these quizzes has been strongly skewed to the left with a mean of $9$ correct answers and a standard deviation of about $2.5$ correct answers.

Suppose we took random samples of $4$ students and calculated $\bar x$ as the sample mean number of questions that the students answered correctly. We can assume that the students in each sample are independent.

**What would be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Since the population of questions answered correctly is skewed to the left, and our sample size of $4$ students is small $(n<30)$, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x $ will still be skewed left but will be approaching a normal distribution.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be skewed to the left, but less skewed than the population.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Dr. Lopez gives students $90$ minutes to complete the final exam for her course. Most students use almost all the time allowed, and relatively few students finish early, so the distribution of times that it takes students to finish the exam is strongly skewed to the left. The mean and standard deviation of the finishing times are $85$ and $10$ minutes, respectively.

Suppose we took random samples of $40$ students and calculated $\bar x$ as the sample mean finishing time. We can assume that the students in each sample are independent.

**What would be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.
Even though the parent population is skewed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal since our sample size of $40$ students is large $(n \geq 30)$.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Teachers in a district have been teaching for a mean of $8$ years with a standard deviation of $3$ years, and the distribution of years teaching is strongly skewed to the right.

Suppose we took random samples of $30$ teachers from this district and calculated $\bar x$ as the sample mean of how long each group of teachers had been teaching. We can assume that the teachers in each sample are independent.

**What would be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.
Even though the parent population is skewed, the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal since our sample size of $30$ teachers is large $(n \geq 30)$.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Geologists recorded the duration (in seconds) of over $500$ recent eruptions of the Old Faithful geyser in Yellowstone National Park. Here's a histogram showing the distribution of their data:

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus duration in seconds. The x axis goes from 75 to 325 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.26. The distribution is irregular and u-shaped between 75 and 325, with a mean of 0.05 at 210 seconds. High points are on either side, 0.23 at 125 seconds and 0.25 at 262 seconds. All values estimated.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7b88e898cc4b176a0d33df8a4a9dc9775644f83e)

Suppose that we were to take random samples of $30$ eruptions from this population and calculate $\bar x$ as the sample mean duration of the eruptions in each sample.

**Which graph shows the most reasonable approximation of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.
Even though the parent population has clusters and gaps of data, the sampling distribution of $\bar x$ will be approximately normal since our sample size of $30$ eruptions is large $(n \geq 30)$.

###Answer

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean in seconds. The x axis goes from 100 to 300 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.32. The distribution is nearly symmetrical between 170 and 250, with mu sub x bar of approximately 0.30 at 210 seconds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/6401c61a9bf2f09f2a71e68e92bd5479645b78db)

choices.content: ![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean in seconds. The x axis goes from 100 to 300 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.20. The distribution is irregular between 100 and 300, with mu sub x bar of approximately 0.19 at 210 seconds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7b1a55ddbdf0a46419f096783713e99796864ba9)

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean in seconds. The x axis goes from 100 to 300 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.21. The distribution is left skewed between 100 and 275 with mu sub x bar of approximately 0.19 at 210 seconds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f68ff418eccd11702bdad46e7a391f6fb62c7c08)

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean in seconds. The x axis goes from 100 to 300 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.32. The distribution is nearly symmetrical between 170 and 250, with mu sub x bar of approximately 0.30 at 210 seconds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8bbc57150f361bf7c15c81695c360090213cbd19)

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean in seconds. The x axis goes from 100 to 300 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.05. The distribution is uniformly distributed between 100 and 300 with mu sub x bar of approximately 0.05 at 200 seconds.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/039af92ae8bf83bb9a250372ebb6b1ab35f2d51a)

assessment_items.question_content:
Census data from a city of $150{,}000$ residents produced the income distribution shown here:

![A distribution curve of income in thousands of dollars. The curve is right skewed with a mean between 50 and 100.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/40330d34499639799fbd1732b564531a43411353)

Suppose that we were to take random samples of $100$ residents from this population and calculate $\bar x$ as the sample mean income of the residents in each sample.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Even though the parent population is skewed to the right, the sampling distribution of sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal since our sample size of $100$ residents is large $(n \geq 30)$.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of $\bar x$ will be approximately normal.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Census data from a city of $80{,}000$ residents produced the income distribution shown here:

![A normal distribution curve of income in thousands of dollars is right skewed with a mean between 60 and 110 thousand dollars.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/57b889569e16bb13cdd68bb3b1f0ce93661dbef3)

Suppose that we were to take random samples of $5$ residents from this population and calculate the sample mean income of the residents in each sample.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Since the population of incomes is skewed to the right, and our sample size of $5$ residents is small $(n<30)$, the sampling distribution of the sample mean will still be right skewed but will be approaching a normal distribution.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean will be skewed to the right, but less so than the population.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
Dr. Virginia Apgar developed a test of infant health that is now known as the Apgar test. It evaluates an infant's health on a scale from $0$ to $10$ across five different indicators—the best possible overall score is $10$ on the test. Here's the distribution of Apgar scores based on over $50{,}000$ birth records:

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus Apgar score. The x axis goes from 0 to 10 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.45. The distribution is left skewed between 1 and 10 with a mode of approximately 0.44 at a score of 9.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c765775b29ce1a171c9707c120236c366ce56980)

Suppose that we took random samples of $9$ infants from this population and calculated the sample mean Apgar score $\bar x$ of the infants in each sample.

**Which graph shows the most reasonable approximation of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Since the population of Apgar scores is skewed to the left, and our sample size of $9$ infants is small $(n<30)$, the sampling distribution of the sample mean will still be left skewed but will be approaching a normal distribution.

###Answer

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean. The x axis goes from 6.8 to 9.2 and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.25. The distribution is left skewed between 6.8 and 9.2, with a mode of approximately 0.23 at 8.5.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7620a5e7208538c8f65677ec5d3cef6d53f9b3c1)

assessment_items.question_content:
The ages of the $175$ players in a youth hockey league have the following distribution:

![A distribution curve of age in years. The curve is left skewed with a median between 12 and 13.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/60b5c325ab9c67af7c4fe8bc4568541fd1a25b31)

Suppose that we were to take random samples of $10$ players from this population and calculate the sample mean age of the players in each sample.

**What will be the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean?**

[[☃ radio 3]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.

Since the population of ages is skewed to the left, and our sample size of $10$ players is small $(n<30)$, the sampling distribution of the sample mean will still be left skewed but will be approaching a normal distribution.

###Answer

The sampling distribution of the sample mean will be skewed to the left, but less so than the population.

choices.content: Unknown; we don't have enough information to determine the shape

assessment_items.question_content:
A professional basketball league records each player's success rate on their free throw attempts. Here is the distribution of success rates for the approximately $400$ players in the league who attempted at least $20$ free throws in a recent season:

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus free throw success rate. The x axis goes from 40% to 100% and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.25. The distribution is left skewed between 45% and 95%, with a mode and mean frequency of approximately 0.22 at 76%.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1e0c997c3da73d708b8f6f130359d0dc9afd1260)

Suppose that we took random samples of $30$ players from this population and calculated the sample mean success rate $\bar x$ of the players in each sample.

**Which graph shows the most reasonable approximation of the sampling distribution of $\bar x$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
###Thinking about population shape and sample size

When a population is not normally distributed, the sampling distribution of $\bar{x}$ depends on the sample size.

For smaller samples $(n<30)$, the shape of the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will match the shape of the parent population, but it will be closer to normal than the parent population is.

For larger samples $(n \geq 30)$, the central limit theorem tells us that the sampling distribution of the sample mean $\bar x$ will be approximately normal regardless of the shape of the parent population.
Even though the parent population is skewed to the left, the sampling distribution of $\bar x$ will be approximately normal since our sample size of $30$ players is large $(n \geq 30)$.

###Answer

![A histogram plots relative frequency versus sample mean. The x axis goes from 66% to 82% and the y axis goes from 0.00 to 0.20. The distribution is roughly symmetrical between 79% and 82% with a mean and mode of approximately 0.18 at 77%.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8174b29de8e002835f2a86da4c6d1d758594f24b)
